pages.raw_text:
George Turner of this city, who


was expected to make a race for


U. S. senator on the democratic


ticket, issued a statement that he
is not to be considered this fall as


candidate for either national or
state office. He says that he has
been in politics for the past 40
years and rest and repose is now
what he wants most Although Mr..
Turner states specifically that he is
not piQued aat the action of the
democratic state convention in
adopting a prohibition plank in the
face of his personal fight against
1t he adds this:


II will not disguise that the ac-
ton of the convention in depart
ing SO far from time-honored demo-
cratic doctrines in it's platform of
principles has confirmed me. so
far as confirmation was necessary,
in my determination. Having op-
posed the adoption of the most
declaration in the plat
form concerning state policy, l am
not the man to lead the democratic
forces Of the state in advocacy of
that policy; moreover, if that
policy shall, in fact, blaze the way
to democratic success, (as was
claimed on the floor of the conven-
ton by it's most distinguished ad-
vocates) then some gentleman in
sympathy with it and responsible
for its adoption is entitled to reap
the fruits of the victory"
